briefdescription.para: Define complex data type ... same as in cntr::

memberdef.argsstring: (herm_matrix_ness &C, int c1, int c2, herm_matrix_ness &A, int a1, int a2, herm_matrix_ness &B, int b1, int b2)
memberdef.name: Bubble1_ness

para.bold: Calculate the bubble diagram of first type: $ C_{c_1,c_2} (t)= i A_{a_1,a_2} (t) B_{b_2,b_1} (-t) $

blockquote.para: Evaluate Green's function C given by the bubble diagram equation $ C_{c_1,c_2} (t)= i A_{a_1,a_2} (t) B_{b_2,b_1} (-t) $. The evaluation is done for all time points and for the lesser and retarded component.

parameterdescription.para: Green's function to store result in

memberdef.argsstring: (herm_matrix_ness &C, int c1, int c2, herm_matrix_ness &A, int a1, int a2, herm_matrix_ness &B, int b1, int b2)
memberdef.name: Bubble2_ness

para.bold: Calculate the bubble diagram of second type: $ C_{c_1,c_2} (t)= i A_{a_1,a_2} (t) B_{b_1,b_2} (t) $

blockquote.para: Evaluate Green's function C given by the bubble diagram equation $ C_{c_1,c_2} (t)= i A_{a_1,a_2} (t) B_{b_1,b_2} (t) $. The evaluation is done for all time points and for the lesser ad retarded component.

parameterdescription.para: Time Green's function to store result in

briefdescription.para: Convert CNTR Green's function cntr::herm_matrix to NESS ness2::herm_matrix_ness by sampling a given time slice.

detaileddescription.para: Read $ G_{\rm ness}^{R|<}(t) = G{\rm cntr}^{R|<}(tstp, tstp - t) $ from CNTR object at time slice tstp, and write to NESS object.
Values at negative times $ t < 0 $ are set by Hermitian symmetry: $ G^<(-t) = -[G^<(t)]^\dagger $
If tstp is smaller than the maximum time in Gness, the tail is left zero.

parameterdescription.para: Type of the contour Green's function (cntr::herm_matrix,cntr::herm_matrix_timestep, cntr::herm_matrix_moving, cntr::herm_matrix_timestep_moving)
Target steady-state Green's function (time domain filled)
Time slice in Gcntr to sample from (defaults to max time)

memberdef.argsstring: (fft_array &G, int nt, Integrand &integrand, double lo, double hi, double h, int nn, int limit)
memberdef.name: compute_lesser

memberdef.argsstring: (cdmatrix &result, int bosefermi, herm_matrix_ness &A, herm_matrix_ness &B, double h)
memberdef.name: convolution_density_matrix

briefdescription.para: Compute the equal-time lesser component of the product $ i (A \cdot B)^< $ for two steady-state Green's functions in NESS representation.

detaileddescription.para: The function transforms A and B to frequency (overwriing eisting values) on the frequency grid of the NESS object, limited to the range

parameterdescription.para: Complex matrix of dimension (size1 x size1) containing the equal-time value $ i(A \cdot B)^<(t=0) $.
(+1) for bosonic functions, (-1) for fermionic functions
Time step used for the Fourier transforms.

briefdescription.para: Compute the equal-time density matrix from the lesser component of a NESS Green's function.

detaileddescription.para: This routine evaluates \[ \rho = {\tt bosefermi} \cdot i \, A^<(t=0) \] where $ A^<(t) $ is the lesser Green's function in the time domain, and $t=0$ denotes the equal-time value.

parameterdescription.para: Complex matrix of dimension (size1 x size1) containing $ {\tt bosefermi} \cdot i A^<(t=0) $.
(+1) for bosonic functions, (-1) for fermionic functions
Steady-state Green's function (herm_matrix_ness) in the time domain.

para.bold: Compute Euclidean distance between two fft_array objects in time or frequency domain

blockquote.para: Computes the L2 norm (Euclidean distance) $ \|A - B\|_2 = \sqrt{ \sum_i |A_i - B_i|^2 } $ between two fft_array objects in either time or frequency domain.

parameterdescription.para: fft_domain::time or fft_domain::freq – determines which internal data to compare
If array sizes or shapes do not match

para.bold: Compute Euclidean distance between two herm_matrix_ness objects

blockquote.para: Computes the combined L2 norm for retarded and lesser components: $ \|A - B\|_2 = \sqrt{ \|A^{ret} - B^{ret}\|_2^2 + \|A^{les} - B^{les}\|_2^2 } $

parameterdescription.para: fft_domain::time or fft_domain::freq – domain to compare

simplesect.para: Total Euclidean norm of the difference across components

briefdescription.para: Downsampling of a fft_array to a coarser time grid, taking only every factor entry of the original object.

detaileddescription.para: The new length is $ N_{ft1} = \frac{N_{ft}}{\tt factor} $. In frequency space, only values $ 0, \ldots, \frac{N_{ft1}}{2} - 1, N_{ft} - \frac{N_{ft1}}{2}, \ldots, N_{ft} - 1 $ of the input are written to the output object. Returns a downsampled fft_array object. It is required that $ N_{ft} $ is an integer multiple of factor, and $ N_{ft1} $ (the new length) is still an integer multiple of 2.

briefdescription.para: Downsampling of a herm_matrix_ness to a coarser time grid, taking only every factor entry of the original object.

detaileddescription.para: The new length is $ N_{ft1} = \frac{N_{ft}}{\tt factor} $. In frequency space, only values $ 0, \ldots, \frac{N_{ft1}}{2} - 1, N_{ft} - \frac{N_{ft1}}{2}, \ldots, N_{ft} - 1 $ of the input are written to the output object. Returns a downsampled herm_matrix_ness object. It is required that $ N_{ft} $ is an integer multiple of factor, and $ N_{ft1} $ (the new length) is still an integer multiple of 2.

memberdef.argsstring: (herm_matrix_ness &G, double mu, cdmatrix &epsilon, herm_matrix_ness &Sigma, double h, fft_integral_method method=FFT_TRAPEZ, dyson_bath_type bath_type=BATH_NONE, double bath_eta=0, double bath_beta=0, double bath_mu=0)
memberdef.name: dyson

briefdescription.para: Solve the Dyson equation for the steady-state Green's function.

blockquote.para: Computes the time-domain solution $ G^{R|<}(t) $ of the Dyson equation for a steady-state system, using a frequency-domain method with optional bath regularization. The method transforms $ \Sigma^{R|<}(t) $ to frequency space, solves Dyson equations there, and back-transforms to obtain $ G^{R|<}(t) $.

parameterdescription.para: Output Green's function $ G^{R|<}(t) $ of type herm_matrix_ness, modified in-place.
Complex-valued single-particle Hamiltonian matrix $ \epsilon $ (dimension must match G).
Input self-energy $ \Sigma^{R|<}(t) $ of type herm_matrix_ness, defined on the same grid as G.
Time step size $ h $ used for time grids.
Integral transformation method (default = FFT_TRAPEZ). Currently only FFT_TRAPEZ is implemented.

Type of bath regularization added to the self-energy. If bath_type != REG_NONE, a bath self-energy $ \Sigma_{\rm reg} $ is added to $ \Sigma $:
REG_CONST: Constant density (Lorentzian broadening)
REG_GAUSS: Gaussian cutoff in frequency space.
REG_OHMIC: Ohmic spectral function with cutoff.
Broadening parameter $ \eta $ for the bath (used for all regularization types except BATH_NONE).
Inverse temperature $ \beta $ of the bath (used in BATH_CONST, BATH_GAUSS, BATH_OHMIC).
Chemical potential $ \mu_{\rm bath} $ of the bath (used in BATH_CONST, BATH_GAUSS).

memberdef.argsstring: (herm_matrix_ness &G, herm_matrix_ness &Sigma, cdmatrix &H, double mu, double h, dyson_bath_type bath_type, double bath_eta, double bath_beta, double bath_mu)
memberdef.name: dyson_fft

memberdef.argsstring: (int bosefermi, herm_matrix_ness &G, DOS &dos, double beta, double mu, double h, fft_integral_method method, int limit=100, int nn=20)
memberdef.name: green_equilibrium_ness

detaileddescription.para: Constructs a retarded and lesser Green's function in the time domain for a system in thermal equilibrium, using a specified density of states and numerical Fourier integration method.

DOS
A density of states class providing:
double lo_ — lower frequency bound
double hi_ — upper frequency bound
double operator()(double omega) — density of states $ A(\omega) $
bose_fermi
= BOSON (+1) or FERMION (-1) sign
G
Output: Green's function object (herm_matrix_ness) to be filled in time domain
dos
Density of states object used to define the spectral function
beta
Inverse temperature $ \beta $
h
Time step size
mu
Chemical potential $ \mu $
method
Integration method to use for the Fourier transform. One of:
FFT_TRAPEZ: Simple piecewise-constant quadrature (trapezoidal rule)
FFT_ADAPTIVE: Adaptive quadrature as in libcntr (uses nn and limit)
nn
Number of sub-sample points per interval (used only if method == FFT_ADAPTIVE)
limit
Maximum number of intervals in adaptive Fourier transform (used only if method == FFT_ADAPTIVE)
Computes the time-domain Green's functions: \[G^R(t) = -i \int_{\mathrm{lo}}^{\mathrm{hi}} d\omega\, A(\omega) e^{-i \omega t} \] \[G^<(t) = - \xi i \int_{\mathrm{lo}}^{\mathrm{hi}} d\omega\, A(\omega) f_F(\omega - \mu) e^{-i \omega t}, \quad f_F(x) = \frac{1}{e^{\beta x} - \xi} \] where $\xi$ = BOSE or FERMI, and $ A(\omega)$ is computed from the density of states.
The integration bounds are taken from dos.lo_ to dos.hi_.
For FFT_ADAPTIVE, a frequency-adaptive scheme is used, refining the interval until convergence. For FFT_TRAPEZ the FFT grid defined by Nft_ and h is used, nn and limit are ignored

memberdef.argsstring: (herm_matrix_ness &G, DOS &dos, double beta, double mu, double h, fft_integral_method method, int limit, int nn)
memberdef.name: green_equilibrium_ness_bose

memberdef.argsstring: (herm_matrix_ness &G, DOS &dos, double beta, double mu, double h, fft_integral_method method, int limit, int nn)
memberdef.name: green_equilibrium_ness_fermi

memberdef.argsstring: (herm_matrix_ness &G, DosFunction &dos, double h, int nn, int limit)
memberdef.name: green_equilibrium_ret_ness_adaptive

briefdescription.para: Convert a steady-state Green's function into a CNTR Green's function, assuming tranlstional invariance in time.

detaileddescription.para: For each pair $ (t, t') $, if the relative time $ t - t' $ exists in the NESS domain, use $ G_{cntr}(t,t') = G_{ness}(t - t') $. Else, set to zero.

parameterdescription.para: Any writable two-time Green's function (cntr::herm_matrix,cntr::herm_matrix_timestep, cntr::herm_matrix_moving, cntr::herm_matrix_timestep_moving)

briefdescription.para: Automatic upsampling of a herm_matrix_ness by a factor of '-factor' if a negative value is passed and an automatic downsampling ba a factor of 'factor' if a positive value is passed.

parameterdescription.para: Coarse-graining factor if positive, if negative '-factor' is fine-graining factor.

memberdef.argsstring: (double &ret, double &les, double omega, double h, dyson_bath_type bath_type, double bath_eta, double bath_beta, double bath_mu)
memberdef.name: sigma_bath

briefdescription.para: Upsampling of a herm_matrix_ness to a finer time grid by transforming to frequency space, appending zeros until the new length $ N_{ft1} = N_{ft} \times factor $, and transforming back.
